semantic_sweep.review_note: Reviewed the complete chapter, all ten bounded source mappings, exact 3/10 plan-execution, 2/7 delivery, and 2/9 durable-lifecycle fixture families, one accepted no-promotion decision, all twenty-seven Lean declarations under five public targets, every manifest atom, every prose candidate, test plan, source crosswalk, summary, and handoff. The sweep made admitted work lifecycle and terminal receipt custody the distinct owner; separated intent, planning, permissions, approvals, runtime effects, context, artifacts, verification, evidence, support, learning, cost, and release; required exact contract/plan parentage, stable job/attempt/lease/effect identities, admission, locks and expiry, typed lifecycle, least privilege, approval, workspace and secret boundaries, scheduling and fairness, leases, observed effects, retry/idempotency, recovery and compensation, delivery/evidence separation, terminal receipts, complete denominators, natural matched baselines, causal ablations, reproduction, and transfer; classified the proof scaffold as two predicate-projection or blocking consequences, twenty-three finite route or negative-case consequences, and two assumption-restating authored-summary bridges; and preserved zero chapter-core support effect.

scope.population: Authorized natural multi-step AI work units from accepted contract and dispatchable plan-node request through admission, queueing, leasing, execution, retry, recovery, adjudication, delivery, evidence-readiness eligibility, failure, quarantine, cancellation, compensation, expiry, or retirement, including every effect, artifact, cost, human decision, and residual.
scope.environment: Ten inspected assigned sources; 3 valid/10 rejecting authored plan-execution fixtures; 2 valid/7 rejecting authored delivery traces; 2 valid/9 rejecting authored durable-lifecycle traces; one accepted no-promotion decision; twenty-seven finite Lean declarations; and a future natural multi-backend, multi-scheduler, multi-model campaign. No scheduler, worker, permission service, approval service, adapter runner, external-effect observer, durable workflow engine, natural outcome, or transfer result is represented.
scope.model: A typed job control-plane relation over contract/plan parentage, job and attempt identities, admission, locks, lifecycle, permissions, approvals, workspaces, adapters, scheduler state, leases, checkpoints, observed effects, retries, recovery, compensation, artifacts, adjudication, delivery, evidence-readiness eligibility, terminal receipts, cost, feedback, expiry, retirement, and residuals; current implementations are a narrow schema, authored validators, and finite route theorems rather than a deployed Labor OS.
scope.authority: Intent and Contracts own objectives; Planning owns plan correctness; Proof and Review supply bounded constraints; Security, Privacy, Rights, Licensing, and accountable humans own grants and approvals; Resource Economics and scheduler policy own capacity/fairness/cost; Runtime Adapters own effects; Context owners own input state; Artifact Graphs own outputs and receipts; Verification, Evidence States, and Claim Ledgers own adequacy and support; learning owners own updates; Readiness and Publication own release. Labor OS owns admitted job lifecycle and receipt custody only and grants no permission, support, learning, or release authority.
scope.time: Semantic review current through 2026-07-14; material contract, plan, input, adapter, policy, threat, environment, authority, rights, consumer, deadline, approval, permission, or lease changes expire affected locks and decisions while preserving history.
scope.artifact: Chapter and manifest contract; ten bounded source mappings; public typed-job schema; plan-execution, delivery, and durable-lifecycle fixture results; accepted no-promotion decision; twenty-seven declarations in lean/AsiStackProofs/TypedJobs.lean; future scheduler/worker/permission/approval/workspace/adapter/effect/artifact/recovery/action/outcome traces, reproduction locks, and transfer artifacts.

assumptions: A bounded work unit can be frozen from an accepted contract and dispatchable plan node before execution outcomes.
Actual permissions, approvals, leases, effects, artifacts, receipts, costs, failures, and residuals can be observed closely enough to distinguish declared from performed work.

Strong chat, ambient-agent, swarm, script, queue, DAG, durable-workflow, process-engine, batch-job, and governed routes can be matched on work, models, tools, authority, resources, faults, and horizons.

Independent-enough evaluators can label authorization, lifecycle correctness, side-effect fidelity, duplicate effects, recovery, compensation, evidence readiness, usefulness, harm, fairness, privacy, human burden, and delayed outcomes.
Runtime effect execution, artifact reality, evidence acceptance, support, learning, and release remain separable from job-record completeness.
Failures, retries, human interventions, unobserved effects, false refusal, missed help, privacy exposure, delays, costs, and residuals remain available for denominator reconciliation.
chapter_defaults.strongest_counterclaim: Mature queues, schedulers, DAG orchestrators, durable workflow runtimes, process engines, batch controllers, capability systems, audit logs, and capable human operators already provide the useful execution substrate. A Labor OS adds metadata and ceremony without enforcing authority or observing hidden effects; retries, approvals, and receipts may increase latency, false refusal, privacy exposure, and human burden while a simpler script or workflow system performs as well.
chapter_defaults.falsifier: A job, parent, approval, permission, lease, attempt, effect, artifact, cost, failure, compensation, delivery state, residual, or irreversible consequence can disappear or silently widen while the record remains conformant; a delivered or evidence-ready record masks unauthorized or incorrect effects; or strong matched simpler workflow routes dominate governed typed jobs on useful completion, authorization, effect fidelity, recovery, fairness, privacy, latency, human burden, and total cost.

required_lanes.necessity: Freeze Talos, VIEA, GenesisCode, Software Magic, MoECOT, Temporal, Airflow, BPMN, Kubernetes, chat, agents, swarms, scripts, queues, DAGs, durable workflows, process engines, and batch controllers without importing source-reported performance.

Justify job admission, authority, permissions, approvals, prioritization, fairness, preemption, privacy, secret use, disclosure, retention, intervention, compensation, irreversible effects, human roles, evidence eligibility, and release boundaries.

Specify typed states, attempts, leases, locks, authority, effects, retry/idempotency, faults, recovery, compensation, receipts, expiry, and countermodels beyond trusted Booleans and authored route order.
Run actual scheduler, worker, workspace, permission, approval, adapter, effect observation, checkpoint, retry, crash, recovery, compensation, artifact, delivery, and retirement paths.

Measure authorized useful completion, unobserved and unauthorized effects, lifecycle and receipt correctness, duplicates, recovery, compensation, evidence-readiness error, false refusal, missed help, fairness, privacy, latency, human work, and total cost jointly.

Ablate parent locks, permissions, approvals, workspace isolation, leases, idempotency, effect observation, recovery, delivery/evidence separation, terminal receipts, and complete denominators under matched work and resources.
Reproduce from locks and transfer across models, domains, tools, backends, schedulers, organizations, authority regimes, permission systems, fault schedules, workload lengths, and time.

contrary_evidence: Temporal-like durable workflows, Airflow-like DAGs, BPMN/process engines, Kubernetes batch jobs, queues, schedulers, capability systems, and ordinary audit logs already implement important execution and recovery properties.
The current schema omits many expanded fields, and all three fixture families are authored zero-model records rather than observed job execution.
The twenty-seven Lean declarations consist of two predicate projections, twenty-three finite route or negative cases, and two authored-summary bridges; they do not refine a scheduler or runtime.
A complete job record can be consistently wrong when permissions, approvals, effects, artifacts, receipts, or compensation are self-reported or incompletely observed.
Durable execution can preserve the wrong contract; typed work can still be unauthorized, useless, harmful, unfair, privacy-expensive, or unrecoverable.
Additional gates can increase latency, false refusal, missed help, starvation, expert burden, and cost without improving useful outcomes.
No Temporal, Airflow, BPMN, Kubernetes, Talos, MoECOT, GenesisCode, or VIEA runtime is executed or reproduced here.
known_confounds: The governed route may receive more metadata, tools, worker capacity, retries, checkpoints, approvals, human intervention, observation hooks, or time than simpler baselines.
Workloads may favor easily typed, replayable, idempotent, reversible, or low-latency jobs and omit open-ended or irreversible failures.
Scheduler, worker, adapter, effect observer, artifact verifier, receipt checker, and outcome evaluator may share code, infrastructure, organizations, incentives, or logs.
Queue status, tool return, file existence, artifact digest, replay success, user acceptance, and green validators can be mistaken for useful authorized completion.

Retries, lease losses, partial effects, compensation failures, rejected jobs, starvation, human repairs, privacy exposure, false refusal, missed help, costs, and residuals can disappear from denominators.
Conservative non-dispatch or non-delivery can reduce observed harm while increasing missed help and making the governed route look safer.
Short horizons can miss duplicated external effects, cleanup failure, stale approvals, revocation, downstream artifacts, delayed harm, and compensation gaps.
acceptance_criteria: Every job, parent, attempt, lease, workspace, approval, permission, adapter, effect, artifact, receipt, cost, failure, recovery, compensation, delivery, evidence-eligibility, feedback, expiry, retirement, and residual has an exact producer, consumer, valid case, rejecting mutation, observed consequence, and replay path.

A prospectively registered natural campaign meets joint useful-completion, authorization, effect-fidelity, lifecycle, duplicate-effect, recovery, compensation, evidence-readiness, false-refusal, missed-help, fairness, privacy, latency, human-work, total-cost, causal, reproduction, and transfer gates against strong matched workflow comparators.

Each mechanism has a predicted causal signature that disappears under matched ablation and cannot be explained by extra resources, easier workloads, hidden human repair, weak baselines, shared observers, denominator changes, template leakage, or non-dispatch.
narrowing_criteria: Narrow every result to exact job family, contract, plan, model, tools, data, permissions, approvals, workspace, adapter, effect class, backend, scheduler, authority regime, resources, fault model, environment, horizon, outcomes, costs, and residuals.

Separate record conformance, lifecycle validity, permission/approval validity, effect observation, artifact reality, recovery, compensation, delivery, evidence readiness, usefulness, support, learning, and release.
Unknown effects, authority, durability, idempotency, compensation, usefulness, fairness, delayed outcome, reproduction, or transfer remains disputed, residual, narrowed, or blocked.
refutation_criteria: Refute an invariant when a reachable ordinary or adversarial trace satisfies its premises and violates parent, identity, lifecycle, authority, approval, permission, lease, effect, retry, recovery, receipt, delivery, evidence, expiry, or denominator preservation.

Refute a mechanism benefit when matched ablation preserves the joint outcome, the mechanism worsens the frontier, or its effect is explained by resources, workload selection, hidden repair, weak baselines, observer dependence, denominator changes, or non-dispatch.

Refute useful, safer, durable, efficient, fair, reproducible, or transferable Labor OS language when unauthorized or hidden effects persist, recovery or compensation fails, costs dominate, a simpler comparator wins, or reproduction and transfer fail.
deprecation_criteria: Deprecate only when a more precise workflow, scheduler, runtime, artifact, evidence, or governance owner supersedes the atom and every job identity, attempt, dependent, effect, artifact, receipt, consumer, and residual migrates with preserved lineage.

residual.next_unblocking_condition: Execute the natural multi-step campaign through actual schedulers, workers, permissions, approvals, workspaces, adapters, effect observers, retries, faults, recovery, compensation, artifacts, delivery and evidence gates against strong matched workflow baselines with complete costs, causal ablations, clean reproduction, and transfer.

non_claims: A typed schema, valid lifecycle row, queue entry, lease, tool return, file, receipt, replay, user delivery, or evidence-ready label does not establish authorization, observed effects, correctness, usefulness, safety, support, learning, or release.
The current 3/10, 2/7, 2/9 fixture families, no-promotion decision, and twenty-seven declarations establish only their exact authored finite boundaries.
No intent, plan, permission, approval, effect, artifact, evidence, support, learning, release, AGI, or ASI state changes through this semantic review.

labor-os-and-typed-jobs.core.strongest_counterclaim: Mature workflow runtimes plus ordinary authority checks already cover this boundary.
labor-os-and-typed-jobs.core.falsifier: No unique exercised consumer or failure remains after owner decomposition, or matched natural execution shows added cost without joint outcome improvement.
acceptance_criteria: Demonstrate natural lifecycle custody, effect fidelity, useful outcomes, complete cost, causal contribution, reproduction, and transfer.

labor-os-and-typed-jobs.problem.001.strongest_counterclaim: Existing task queues and operators already make work inspectable enough.
labor-os-and-typed-jobs.problem.001.falsifier: Matched ordinary workflows show no material authorization, lifecycle, effect, receipt, delivery, or residual failure missed by existing systems.
acceptance_criteria: Predeclare work cases where the typed-job boundary changes an action, recovery, evidence, or release decision.

labor-os-and-typed-jobs.insufficiency.00.strongest_counterclaim: A good agent framework with logs and approvals supplies the same properties.
labor-os-and-typed-jobs.insufficiency.00.falsifier: A strong matched composed agent/workflow baseline preserves every boundary and equals or dominates governed typed jobs.
acceptance_criteria: Implement the strongest composed baseline rather than treating chat or swarms as the only alternative.

labor-os-and-typed-jobs.minimum.001.strongest_counterclaim: Schemas, fixtures, and route theorems do not constitute a Labor OS.
labor-os-and-typed-jobs.minimum.001.falsifier: Any 3/10, 2/7, 2/9, decision, 27-declaration, artifact, or non-claim statement is inaccurate or presented as runtime evidence.
acceptance_criteria: Keep all counts and ceilings exact and require actual natural scheduler/runtime execution before promotion.

labor-os-and-typed-jobs.beyond_sota.001.strongest_counterclaim: The proposal combines mature workflow, scheduler, capability, approval, and audit mechanisms without measurable novelty.
labor-os-and-typed-jobs.beyond_sota.001.falsifier: The governed route fails any preregistered usefulness, authorization, effect, recovery, fairness, cost, causal, reproduction, or transfer gate.
acceptance_criteria: Freeze exact comparators and thresholds before outcomes and report positive, negative, null, narrowed, refuted, and cost-dominated results.

labor-os-and-typed-jobs.invariant.003.strongest_counterclaim: A smaller pending/running/succeeded/failed state set is operationally sufficient.
labor-os-and-typed-jobs.invariant.003.falsifier: State collapse hides a material approval, lease, retry, adjudication, evidence, quarantine, compensation, expiry, or retirement decision.
acceptance_criteria: Exercise each state and invalid transition under ordinary and fault traces with downstream consequences.

labor-os-and-typed-jobs.invariant.005.strongest_counterclaim: Static service accounts and container policies already bound execution adequately.
labor-os-and-typed-jobs.invariant.005.falsifier: Dispatch, retry, resume, delegation, or replay widens a capability, data, secret, network, side-effect, disclosure, retention, or release grant.
acceptance_criteria: Observe actual grants and effects across revocation, retry, resume, delegation, and replay.

labor-os-and-typed-jobs.invariant.009.strongest_counterclaim: Standard retry libraries and workflow engines already handle duplicate effects.
labor-os-and-typed-jobs.invariant.009.falsifier: A retry/resume loses attempt lineage, uses a stale lease, widens authority, duplicates effects, or ignores prior partial outcomes.
acceptance_criteria: Inject timeouts, lost acknowledgments, worker crashes, partial effects, and partitions against multiple delivery semantics.

labor-os-and-typed-jobs.invariant.012.strongest_counterclaim: Successful delivered work is usually good enough to count as evidence.
labor-os-and-typed-jobs.invariant.012.falsifier: Produced or delivered output becomes verified, evidence-ready, supported, or released without the separately owned records.
acceptance_criteria: Trace every state across partial verification, missing artifacts, stale replay, disputes, and delayed outcomes.

labor-os-and-typed-jobs.invariant.014.strongest_counterclaim: Workflow cancellation or rollback is sufficient for practical cleanup.
labor-os-and-typed-jobs.invariant.014.falsifier: A terminal record claims closure while external effects, copies, disclosures, artifacts, obligations, or descendants persist unrecorded.
acceptance_criteria: Audit reachable effects and obligations across cancel, compensate, revoke, expire, cleanup, and retire operations.

labor-os-and-typed-jobs.invariant.017.strongest_counterclaim: Only completed useful jobs matter to system performance.
labor-os-and-typed-jobs.invariant.017.falsifier: Restoring failures, denials, retries, lease loss, interventions, partial effects, compensation, quarantine, human work, privacy, delay, missed help, and residuals changes no conclusion.
acceptance_criteria: Reconcile every admitted and rejected work item through terminal outcome and total cost.

labor-os-and-typed-jobs.failure_mode.003.strongest_counterclaim: Operational status semantics are clear enough to users and systems.
labor-os-and-typed-jobs.failure_mode.003.falsifier: Queueing, leasing, response, output, delivery, or acknowledgement never creates a materially false completion or evidence decision.
acceptance_criteria: Seed incomplete states and audit downstream action, support, and release interpretations.

labor-os-and-typed-jobs.failure_mode.009.strongest_counterclaim: Idempotency keys eliminate material retry duplication.
labor-os-and-typed-jobs.failure_mode.009.falsifier: Timeout, crash, partition, stale input, and lost-ack traces never duplicate or widen a non-idempotent effect.
acceptance_criteria: Use independently observed effect ledgers and adversarial retry schedules across real adapters.

labor-os-and-typed-jobs.failure_mode.010.strongest_counterclaim: Compensating transactions adequately restore workflow state.
labor-os-and-typed-jobs.failure_mode.010.falsifier: No external effect, disclosure, artifact, message, model update, or descendant persists after a declared compensated closure.
acceptance_criteria: Measure effect-complete closure and exact residuals across irreversible and partially reversible actions.

labor-os-and-typed-jobs.formal.jobs-life.strongest_counterclaim: The theorem proves jobs use valid lifecycle transitions.
labor-os-and-typed-jobs.formal.jobs-life.falsifier: The modeled validity Boolean or relation is disconnected from actual scheduler state or omits material transitions.
acceptance_criteria: Prove refinement from typed runtime events to the lifecycle state machine and test fault traces.

labor-os-and-typed-jobs.formal.jobs-life.strongest_counterclaim: The theorem proves approval-required jobs cannot run without approval.
labor-os-and-typed-jobs.formal.jobs-life.falsifier: The approval predicate lacks authority or runtime connection, or a real job dispatches despite missing approval.
acceptance_criteria: Connect approval authority and exact job version to executable admission and independently observed denial.

labor-os-and-typed-jobs.formal.jobs-life.strongest_counterclaim: Complete route enumeration proves the execution controller handles these cases.
labor-os-and-typed-jobs.formal.jobs-life.falsifier: Route order is authored, unreachable, incomplete, or disconnected from runtime events and side effects.
acceptance_criteria: Add typed traces, reachability/countermodels, scheduler refinement, and fault-injected execution.

labor-os-and-typed-jobs.formal.jobs-life.strongest_counterclaim: The bridge proves the delivery probe's evidence-readiness discipline.
labor-os-and-typed-jobs.formal.jobs-life.falsifier: Summary fields are merely asserted, mismatch the JSON artifact, or are narrated as runtime delivery/evidence correctness.
acceptance_criteria: Bind generated traces to typed semantics and prove artifact correspondence before substantive classification.

labor-os-and-typed-jobs.formal.jobs-life.strongest_counterclaim: The bridge proves durable retry and lease behavior.
labor-os-and-typed-jobs.formal.jobs-life.falsifier: Summary fields are merely asserted, mismatch the JSON result, or are narrated as durable workflow recovery.
acceptance_criteria: Bind real scheduler/worker events and effect observations to typed traces before substantive classification.

preserved_disposition.rationale: Wrapped proof-depth classification in the minimum atom.

prose_review_lineage.retirement_reason: The chapter proof surface was rewritten during the active refinement; this sentence is no longer current prose and no current scanner candidate is its semantic replacement.

non_claims: Semantic review and atomization do not promote the Labor OS core or any proof target.
The current validators and Lean build establish finite authored record and route boundaries only.
External workflow documentation supplies comparator lineage, not local scheduler, integration, or runtime evidence.
